title: WOS Studio (Authoring) — Common $defs Library
description: Shared $defs library for the Studio Stage-3 schema family. Carries the canonical enums that govern authoring discipline (OriginClass, MappingState, the per-entity lifecycle/review states) plus shared helpers (AuthorityGrantApplied, Iri). No document marker; this schema is registered by $id only and consumed by every other studio schema via $ref. The previously-exported StudioMetadataEnvelope was removed in Wave 1 of the Stage 3 + 4 review remediation (2026-05-02): the envelope is conceptual, not a structurally-inherited type — per-host inlining is the actual contract. See studio-authoring/CONCEPT-MODEL.md §6.1 (composition strategy) for the design.
$comment: Promotion status (Stage-3, 2026-05-01): MVP shapes; field lists are illustrative, not exhaustive. Inner fields may evolve as Stage-3 hardens; structural conformance to envelope/originClass/mappingState is load-bearing now.

OriginClass.description: Origin classification per authoring-provenance.md. Every approved PolicyObject + WorkflowIntent element MUST carry exactly one originClass: `source` (lifted directly from a SourceCitation), `approved-interpretation` (reviewer-judged interpretation of source), `local-practice` (workspace-specific operational practice with no upstream source), `assumption` (explicit gap-fill backed by an Assumption PolicyObject), or `runtime-observed` (proposed by a runtime-observation cluster — Phase-4 reserved seam).

MappingState.description: Mapping disposition per studio-to-wos-mapping.md. Precedence: mapsToWos > authoringOnly > requiresSpecExtension > unmappedButApproved. The four states encode the authoring-vocabulary-vs-substrate split (see CM §5 'WOS as canonical substrate').

AuthorityGrantApplied.description: Reference to an AuthorityGrant consulted during a permission decision. Records the grant id + the action it authorized.

grantRef.description: AuthorityGrant id from the workspace policy.

exercisedBy.description: Subject id (per identity-and-attestation.md) who exercised the authority.

Iri.description: Generic IRI (Internationalized Resource Identifier). Used for canonical term references, DPV terms, LegalRuleML terms, etc. Not enum-constrained — open vocabulary.
